instruction: In ~/Desktop/finance_exports, move only the .csv files from the top level into ~/Desktop/finance_exports/csv_ready. Leave .txt files and nested files untouched. Then create ~/Desktop/finance_exports/csv_ready/manifest.txt listing the moved csv filenames in alphabetical order, one per line.
source: 外部参考来源：Canonical tutorial - Command line for beginners (https://ubuntu.com/tutorials/command-line-for-beginners)

parameters.command:
rm -rf ~/Desktop/finance_exports && mkdir -p ~/Desktop/finance_exports/nested && printf 'a
' > ~/Desktop/finance_exports/jan.csv && printf 'b
' > ~/Desktop/finance_exports/feb.csv && printf 'notes
' > ~/Desktop/finance_exports/readme.txt && printf 'nested
' > ~/Desktop/finance_exports/nested/mar.csv

result.command: jan_ok='false'; [ -f ~/Desktop/finance_exports/csv_ready/jan.csv ] && jan_ok='true'; feb_ok='false'; [ -f ~/Desktop/finance_exports/csv_ready/feb.csv ] && feb_ok='true'; no_txt='false'; [ ! -f ~/Desktop/finance_exports/csv_ready/readme.txt ] && no_txt='true'; txt_kept='false'; [ -f ~/Desktop/finance_exports/readme.txt ] && txt_kept='true'; nested_kept='false'; [ -f ~/Desktop/finance_exports/nested/mar.csv ] && nested_kept='true'; expected=$(printf 'feb.csv\njan.csv\n'); actual=$(cat ~/Desktop/finance_exports/csv_ready/manifest.txt 2>/dev/null); manifest_ok='false'; [ "$actual" = "$expected" ] && manifest_ok='true'; echo "jan_ok=$jan_ok"; echo "feb_ok=$feb_ok"; echo "no_txt=$no_txt"; echo "txt_kept=$txt_kept"; echo "nested_kept=$nested_kept"; echo "manifest_ok=$manifest_ok"
